sanskrit:
यत्र काले त्वनावृत्तिमावृत्तिं चैव योगिनः।
प्रयाता यान्ति तं कालं वक्ष्यामि भरतर्षभ।।
transliteration:
yatra kāle tv anāvṛttim āvṛttiṁ caiva yoginaḥ
prayātā yānti taṁ kālaṁ vakṣyāmi bharatarṣabha
literal_meaning:
At which time the yogis departing obtain no-return or return—
that time I shall declare to you, O best of the Bharatas.
contextual_meaning: Krishna signals that the moment of transition (death) is not random; its psychological ‘timing’ determines whether the mind-stream loops back into rebirth or attains closure.  The verse introduces a taxonomy of exit-points that mirror inner readiness rather than clock time.
psychological_state: Pre-contemplation stage of existential transition—aware that the quality of one’s dying moment is shaped by lifelong mental habits, yet still seeking external coordinates for an essentially internal process.
trigger_scenario: A middle-aged professional told ‘you have six good months’ who obsessively researches euthanasia clinics, astrology apps and afterlife testimonials instead of examining the quality of presence he brings to each remaining day.
anchor_text: Die when the mind is still and you won’t come back.
